name: stress_ramp
_dataset_notes: 2 teachers, 4 classrooms each, 25 students = 200 students total.
Benchmark tests teacher 1 only (100 students across 4 classrooms).
Hot data: 100 students x 2 hot weeks x 4 runs x 6 turns = ~4800 hot turns.
Cold rollup tables hold 8 weeks of compacted history.
This data volume is intentionally heavier than realistic_school_day to stress analytics queries.
_traffic_notes: ramp_mode: true makes all scenarios use ramping-arrival-rate from 0 to the target rate over the full duration.
Target rates are the PEAK at end of ramp: 8 ingest + 4 dashboard + 3 analytics + 2 turn_insights + 2 replay = 19 req/sec.
The ramp reveals WHERE latency starts climbing and WHERE drops first appear.
Expect dropped_iterations toward the end of the ramp - that is the point of this scenario.
Compare the ramp report's latency-over-time against realistic_school_day to understand the server's headroom.
VUs are sized to handle the peak rates plus expected response time under load (Little's Law with 3x headroom).
